description_long: This object is a vintage tabletop radio receiver with a rectangular, slightly curved body finished in a dark, glossy wood or Bakelite material. The front panel features a combination of wood and metallic textures, with a large circular tuning dial on the right, surrounded by a frequency indicator ring, and three round control knobs below. To the left of the dial is a fabric-covered speaker grille, bordered by a metallic strip, and a small green indicator light above. The back panel is a flat, brown fiberboard with printed diagrams and port labels, typical of mid-20th-century radios. The sides and top are smooth and unadorned, emphasizing the classic, streamlined design. This radio receiver would be found in urban interiors, antique shops, or as a decorative piece in retro-themed environments.
description: A vintage tabletop radio receiver with a dark, glossy body, a large circular tuning dial, three control knobs, and a fabric speaker grille on the front.
description_view_0: The view shows the smooth, dark, and slightly curved side of the radio receiver, with no visible controls or features.
description_view_1: The front view displays the main features: a large circular tuning dial, three control knobs, a fabric speaker grille, a metallic strip, and a small green indicator light.
description_view_2: This view mirrors view 0, showing the opposite smooth, dark side of the radio receiver, again with no visible features.
description_view_3: The back view reveals a flat, brown fiberboard panel with printed diagrams and port labels, typical of vintage radios.
